news.title: Beyond buildings: How architects are shaping sustainability and policy today
news.para: Architects now contribute significantly to India's development and urban planning.  Their interdisciplinary knowledge enhances their influence on decision-making processes.  Growing public demand for quality spaces expands the architect's professional scope.  Sustainability and policy development are key areas where architects offer expertise.  This evolving role positions architecture to shape India's future settlements.

news.title: As India's energy revolution accelerates, real estate stands to gain
news.para: Energy access is shifting from security to national sovereignty for nations. Real estate infrastructure converts energy potential into sustained economic value for India. Industrial parks and renewable energy facilities create economic epicenters and ecosystems. India's policy architecture supports energy ambition and infrastructure execution. Real estate is the ground for India's energy sovereignty and global standing.

news.title: Rajasthan: 86 colonies plan mahapanchayat over pending land titles
news.para: Residents of 86 colonies in Sanganer and Bagru are demanding land titles.  These families established their homes decades ago through cooperative societies.  They warn of boycotting local body elections if demands are unmet.  A mahapanchayat and public movement are planned if no decision occurs.  The Niyaman Hetu Sangharsh Samiti leads this ongoing community effort.

news.title: Mumbai: BMC plans to launch single portal for construction waste monitoring by July-end
news.para: Mumbai's BMC will launch an integrated construction and demolition waste management portal by July.  This new system aims to monitor all waste generated within the city.  A soil exchange platform will also be introduced for developers and contractors.  Government agencies and infrastructure projects will register on this single digital platform.  The portal will prevent illegal dumping and ensure waste accountability across Mumbai.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh government approves Visakhapatnam's master plan 2041
news.para: The Andhra Pradesh government has approved the revised VMRDA Master Plan-2041. This plan incorporates several key development proposals for regional growth. It includes major infrastructure initiatives and economic development projects. The plan also integrates environmental safeguards and connectivity improvements. This framework guides the Visakhapatnam Metropolitan Region's future development until 2041.

news.title: Panchkula civic body flags ₹37 crore property tax dues
news.para: Panchkula Municipal Corporation identified property tax arrears totaling Rs 37.68 crore.  These outstanding dues are from 697 properties with payments exceeding two lakh rupees.  Private properties owe Rs 25.95 crore, while government entities owe Rs 11.73 crore.  Despite a waiver, only Rs 5.92 crore was recovered by the civic body.  A 75% interest rebate is now offered until August 31.

news.title: Kolkata: Only 11 out of 136 high-rise sites surveyed get construction go-ahead
news.para: A government committee has cleared only eleven of 136 surveyed buildings.  Many projects remain stalled due to non-compliance with labor registration requirements.  Officials are inspecting worker facilities and safety procedures at construction sites.  Developers often shift responsibility for labor compliance to contractors.  The Credai will urge developers to adhere to labor laws for project clearance.

news.title: Why 24,000 flats not offered to slum dwellers facing eviction near PM residence: Delhi HC
news.para: Delhi High Court questioned authorities about relocating slum dwellers 45 kms away. Over 24,000 constructed homes for evictees remain unused and in disrepair. The court demanded answers on why these vacant houses are not available. Authorities spent nearly Rs 2,000 crore on these unused rehabilitation units. The court emphasized the rights of petitioners under Article 21.

news.title: MHADA lottery on hold... for want of a VIP
news.para: MHADA homebuyers face prolonged uncertainty as the lottery draw date remains unannounced.  The housing authority is still trying to finalize a senior political leader as chief guest.  This delay has caused significant anxiety for thousands of hopeful applicants.  MHADA has already extended application deadlines and postponed the draw multiple times.  The authority plans to announce the lottery date soon, even without a minister.

news.title: HSVP eyes 196 acres for housing hub near Aravalis
news.para: Gurgaon HSVP seeks 196 acres from landowners for a new residential sector. Shikohpur village offers the largest parcel of 107 acres for this planned development. This acquisition aligns with Haryana's policy for voluntary land procurement for urban growth. Approval is pending at HSVP headquarters before negotiations can commence. Future development will adhere to environmental norms and planning regulations.

news.title: Haryana RERA asks Godrej to hand over delayed flats, pay interest
news.para: Gurgaon's real estate regulator ordered Godrej Highview to give possession of a flat.  The developer must also pay statutory delay possession interest to the buyers.  Buyers had booked the apartment in 2018, with possession due in 2023.  The regulator rejected the developer's force majeure claims for further extensions.  Godrej Highview is now directed to deliver the unit after obtaining the occupation certificate.

news.title: Karnataka RERA recovers ₹1.5 crore from builders over disclosure violations
news.para: Karnataka Rera recovered Rs 1.5 crore from builders for delayed reports. This action enforces compliance and enhances transparency for homebuyers. Builders face penalties for not submitting mandatory quarterly progress and annual audit reports. The authority monitors project timelines and progress through these essential filings. Homebuyers welcomed the stricter enforcement of reporting requirements.

news.title: Maharashtra withdraws 2023 circular on minor mineral levy during land development
news.para: Maharashtra government withdrew a May 2023 circular on minor mineral royalty. This move provides relief to developers facing legal disputes over the framework. The earlier policy faced challenges for inconsistency with court judgments. A fresh, transparent framework will be framed after considering judicial directions. Until then, a new procedure for excavation permissions will be issued.

news.title: NGT halts all constructions inside Ghaziabad city forest
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered the Ghaziabad district magistrate to stop illegal construction. This directive came after a plea alleged damage to Sai Upwan city forest. The tribunal noted that arguments from the applicant remained uncontroverted. Sai Upwan is a notified city forest under Ghaziabad Master Plans. The next hearing for this significant environmental matter is scheduled for September 16.

news.title: UP-RERA issues guidelines for refund of excess GST collected from homebuyers
news.para: UP-RERA has clarified the procedure for refund of excess GST collected from allottees in registered real estate projects. It has directed promoters to collect GST only at rates notified by the government.

news.title: Saligao residents slam cancelled inspection of housing project
news.para: Saligao residents allege authorities are avoiding action on construction violations. They have pointed out structural violations and misrepresentation of facts for months. A scheduled joint inspection was cancelled, further agitating the concerned residents. Residents expressed anger towards the local MLA for alleged inaction and betrayal. They have urged authorities for nearly a year to halt the ongoing construction work.

news.title: SC freezes bank accounts of Parsvnath Developers, directors; issues warrants against its officials
news.para: The Supreme Court froze Parsvnath Developers' bank accounts and issued warrants.  This action followed a twenty-year struggle by senior citizens for their homes.  The court criticized Haryana authorities for failing to enforce RERA orders.  This case highlights concerns about the Real Estate Act's effectiveness.  The court expressed grave concern over regulatory authority inaction and potential collusion.

news.title: Ranbir Kapoor buys land parcel in Pune’s Mulshi for ₹16.42 crore
news.para: Bollywood actor Ranbir Kapoor has acquired over twenty-five acres of land in Pune's Mulshi taluka. These four adjoining land parcels were purchased for more than sixteen crore rupees. The largest acquisition involved a ten-acre plot bought for over seven crore rupees. Kapoor also recently purchased land in Ayodhya for over three crore rupees. Celebrity real estate investments remain robust across various Indian markets.

news.title: Smartworks leases over 930 seats to professional services firm in Pune
news.para: Smartworks has leased over 930 seats in Pune to the Indian subsidiary of a UK-headquartered professional services and technology firm. The 60-month engagement is expected to generate rental revenue of about ₹58 crore.

news.title: Factories turn into townships as Madhya Pradesh industries build worker housing
news.para: Industries across Madhya Pradesh are building employee housing on factory land. This trend aims to reduce commute times and boost worker retention rates. Companies find quality accommodation is now crucial for attracting skilled manpower. Integrated industrial campuses will support sustained growth in new clusters. This development enhances convenience and industrial efficiency for all.
